records.ety: [OE. veile, OF. veile, F. voile, L. velum a sail, covering, curtain, veil, probably fr. vehere to bear, carry, and thus originally, that which bears the ship on. See Vehicle, and cf. Reveal.]

defs: Something hung up, or spread out, to intercept the view, and hide an object; a cover; a curtain; esp., a screen, usually of gauze, crape, or similar diaphanous material, to hide or protect the face.
quotes: The veil of the temple was rent in twain.
She, as a veil down to the slender waist, Her unadornd golden tresses wore.

defs: A cover; a disguise; a mask; a pretense.
quotes: [I will] pluck the borrowed veil of modesty from the so seeming Mistress Page.

defs: A membrane connecting the margin of the pileus of a mushroom with the stalk; -- called also velum.
A covering for a person or thing; as, a nun's veil; a paten veil; an altar veil.

records.ety: [Cf. OF. veler, F. voiler, L. velarc. See Veil, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Veiled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Veiling.]

defs: To throw a veil over; to cover with a veil.
quotes: Her face was veiled; yet to my fancied sight, Love, sweetness, goodness, in her person shined.

defs: Fig.: To invest; to cover; to hide; to conceal.
quotes: To keep your great pretenses veiled.
